title: Appellate Quarterly - July 2025
content:
## Appellate Quarterly - July 2025
Join McCarthy Tétrault’s National Appellate Litigation Group as we discuss appeals of importance to the Canadian business community during the last quarter. Each session brings you up-to-date on recent appellate developments across Canada. Topics to be discussed this quarter include:
- Supreme Court of Canada : Can the CRTC regulate telecommunications carriers’ access to public property for the purpose of installing 5G small cell antennas?
- Federal : The FCA’s decision on whether the Prime Minister and Minister of Justice can be compelled to appoint judges.
- Ontario : Appellate Contractual Interpretation and Proving Defamation Damages.
- Québec : Québec Court of Appeal clarifies criteria for the suspension of legislation and discusses its jurisdiction to review decisions by a single judge of its court.
- West : Privacy in the Spotlight: Insights from the BCCA.
Speakers include: Brandon Kain , Patrick Williams , Kosta Kalogiros , Sajeda Hedaraly and Kara Smyth . Note: All instructions and information on how to access the webinar will be forwarded a few days before the event. This program qualifies for up to 1.5 hours of credit under the mandatory education regimes in British Columbia and Ontario. Barreau du Québec Continuing Education : This program contains up to 1.5 hours of content. For more information regarding the declaration and recognition of educational activities, please contact the Barreau du Québec .
For questions about this event, please contact clientevents@mccarthy.ca .
